data.title: CleanSpark tops crypto firms with $2B+ market cap in short interest as of mid-February
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) witnessed a volatile run in February, with its price reaching as low as $60,230.14 on February 6, its lowest level since the collapse of Sam Bankman-Fried's FTX crypto exchange in late 2022.

data.title: BitGo Holdings gets new Buy rating at Citi
data.description:
Citigroup initiated coverage of BitGo Holdings (BTGO) with a Buy/High Risk rating, highlighting the crypto company's plan to shift toward a broader institutional infrastructure business from its core custody offerings.

The strategy is intended to pull in deeper wallet-to-workflow integrations and generate more revenue from a broader share of client spending. The CLARITY crypto infrastructure bill being hammered out in Congress should also benefit BitGo.

"With CLARITY, we believe BitGo’s role can flourish with the advent of regulated DeFi and tokenization of RWAs – likely affording strategic value to the name," analyst Peter Christiansen wrote in a note to clients.

"We agree with the 'picks and shovels' enabler characterization, though caveat with BitGo’s exchange-like crypto exposure," he added.

The Buy rating is more bullish than the average SA Analyst rating  of Hold.

Still, BitGo (BTGO) stock slid 10% in Tuesday morning trading as bitcoin (BTC-USD) dipped 2.7% to ~$67.0K, not far above its 52-week low of $60.2K.

data.title: Trump administration may change steel and aluminum tariffs, Greer says
data.description:
President Trump's tariffs on steel and aluminum have been "incredibly successful," but he is open to modifying its broad tariffs on steel and aluminum products, U.S. Trade Representative Greer said Tuesday on CNBC.

"You may want to sometimes adjust the way some of the tariffs are applied for compliance purposes," Greer said in a CNBC interview. "We've heard stories of companies that have had to hire extra people for compliance. We're not trying to have people do so much bean counting they're not running their company correctly."

The Trump administration reportedly has been working to narrow the scope of the tariffs, which not only include the metals themselves but also dozens of products that contain them.

Greer said the underlying metals tariffs will remain, calling them "very successful" in boosting the domestic industry. "Clearly, those are going in the right direction. They're going to stay in place," he said in the interview.

Last year, the U.S. imposed a 50% tariff on foreign steel and aluminum, a move that was intended to hit Chinese overcapacity but also wound up impacting other major trading partners hard, including Canada, Mexico, South Korea, and the European Union.

In early trading Monday: Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF) -3.1%, Nucor (NUE) -2.2%, Steel Dynamics (STLD) -2.2%.

ETFs: (SLX), (XME)

data.title: Accenture gets rating upgrade at Wells Fargo
data.description:
Wells Fargo upgraded Accenture's (ACN) rating to Overweight from Equal Weight while maintaining its $275 price target on the stock.

Shares of Accenture rose about 1% on Tuesday. 

Analysts led by Jason Kupferberg said they upgraded the rating on: fiscal second-half revenue acceleration; conservative fiscal 2026 guidance; overblown AI fears; and valuation.

The analysts added that recent booking trends are encouraging, and Accenture could be poised for solid fiscal second-quarter results next month.

The Irish consulting services provider is slated to report its fiscal second-quarter results on March 19.

The analysts noted that they have increased confidence in fiscal second-quarter constant currency revenue growth acceleration, given two consecutive quarters of improved bookings growth, positive incremental discretionary spending commentary from peers, and easier comps.

In addition, the analysts said they view Accenture's fiscal 2026 revenue guidance (which at the high end assumed no improvement in discretionary spending) as conservative. The analysts think the recent AI fears (spilling over from Software) are overblown and added that the valuation is attractive.

"Shares have been excessively punished recently due to latest round of AI fears, spilling over from anti-software sentiment. While ACN is not immune to AI headline risks we see ACN as well-positioned to emerge as a net beneficiary (sticky C-level client relationships, track record of navigating past tech paradigm shifts). CC revs [revenues] growth accel [acceleration] would support this thesis, catalyzing multiple expansion. F1Q AI-related revs/bkgs [revenue/bookings] grew 120%/83% though ACN is discontinuing such disclosures going forward," said Kupferberg and his team.

data.title: GSK says RSV shot Arexvy lowers risk of CV events, asthma, and COPD
data.description:
• GSK (GSK) said that real-world data of its respiratory syncytial virus vaccine Arexvy showed that it led to a reduction in major cardiovascular events, as well as reduced the risk of asthma and COPD flare-ups, compared to those who were unvaccinated. 
  • The retrospective cohort study of more than 2.5M US patients demonstrated 75.6% vaccine effectiveness against RSV-related hospitalization.
  • Other data indicated 63.1% vaccine effectiveness against major  cardiovascular events, such as heart attack and stroke, during RSV-related hospitalization among adults who were at least 60 years old. Also, vaccine effectiveness was, respectively, 74.4% and 61.6%  against severe COPD and asthma flare-ups during RSV-related hospitalization in the same age group.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P 500 in red as Wall Street returns from holiday
data.description:
The stock market searched for direction as Wall Street returned to trading Tuesday after the President’s Day holiday.

S&P 500 (SP500) -0.1%, the Dow (DJI) +0.3%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.4%.

The February Empire State Manufacturing Index came in at 7.1 vs. 7.0 consensus and 7.7 in January, according to data released by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.

U.S. Treasury yields moved lower across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell to 4.03%, down 1.9 basis points. The 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) decreased to 3.40%, while the 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) slipped to 4.67%.

data.title: Most shorted stocks with up to $2B market cap as of mid-February
data.description:
Short sellers are heavily targeting small- and mid-cap names, particularly in the biotech sector, where several stocks are carrying short interest above 30%.

KalVista Pharmaceuticals (KALV) and Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA) lead the list with nearly 40% of their float sold short, underscoring elevated bearish positioning in clinical-stage and high-volatility names. Tango Therapeutics (TNGX), 34.02%, Recursion Pharmaceuticals (RXRX), 32.20%, and Novavax (NVAX), 31.50%, make up other biotech names in the list.

Here’s a list of the top 10 most shorted small - to mid-cap stocks (as a % of shares outstanding) as of February 15:

  • KalVista Pharmaceuticals (KALV) – Short interest: 39.69%
  • Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA) – Short interest: 39.13%
  • C3is (CISS) – Short interest: 34.12%
  • Tango Therapeutics (TNGX) – Short interest: 34.02%
  • Recursion Pharmaceuticals (RXRX) – Short interest: 32.20%
  • Novavax (NVAX) – Short interest: 31.50%
  • Beyond Meat (BYND) – Short interest: 31.11%
  • Kustom Entertainment (KUST) – Short interest: 30.99%
  • Q/C Technologies (QCLS) – Short interest: 30.46%
  • C3.ai (AI) – Short interest: 29.89%

Here’s a list of small- and mid-cap ETFs: iShares Core S&P Small-Cap ETF (IJR), Vanguard Small-Cap ETF (VB), iShares Russell 2000 ETF (IWM), Vanguard Mid-Cap ETF (VO), iShares Core S&P Mid-Cap ETF (IJH), and the SPDR S&P MidCap 400 ETF Trust (MDY).

data.title: American Express Global Business Travel doubles share repurchase program to $600 million
data.description:
• American Express Global Business Travel, operated by Global Business Travel Group (GBTG) has expanded its share repurchase program by $300 million to $600 million.
  • Amex GBT also reported preliminary fourth quarter revenue growth of 34% to $792 million (vs consensus of 789.48M).
  • It sees Total Transaction Value (TTV) growth of 45% to $10 billion and adjusted EBITDA growth of 17% to $130 million.
  • For the full year, revenue is expected to grow 12% to $2.718 billion (vs consensus of 2.71B)
  • Total Transaction Value (TTV) is expected to grow 17% to $36 billion with adjusted EBITDA growth seen at 11% to $532 million.
  • For FY2026 the company has guided revenue growth of 19% to 21%, to a range of $3.235 billion to $3.295 billion. Adjusted EBITDA is seen growing between 16% to 21%, to a range of $615 million to $645 million (vs consensus of 3.25B).
  • GBTG +3.97% premarket to $5.24.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Austral Gold to raise A$8.45M in private placement
data.description:
• Austral Gold (AGLDF) has received A$8.456M of firm commitments for a private placement of new fully paid ordinary shares from Australian investors.
  • The placement involves the issuance of 46.97M new shares at A$0.18 (approximately CDN$0.17) per share to Australian investors. The offer price represents a 20% discount to the closing price on the ASX on February 16, 2026.
  • Austral intends to use the proceeds from the placement to accelerate exploration programs in Guanaco, Chile, and Casposo, Argentina; invest in capital expenditure to expand processing (milling) capacity at Casposo and to increase agitation leaching capacity at Guanaco; and for working capital purposes.

data.title: Citi downgrades Bath & Body Works as FY26 will show cracks in its core business
data.description:
While Bath & Body Works (BBWI) is expected to beat fourth quarter consensus estimates, its full year guidance will likely reflect continued headwinds to its core business ahead of a rollout on Amazon (AMZN) and efforts to drive traffic through increased promotions.

Accordingly, Citi Research analyst Paul Lejuez downgraded the stock to Neutral from Buy ahead of fourth quarter results, anticipating that Bath & Body Works (BBWI) will show a fifth consecutive year of sales declines driven by weakness in its core business.

The Disney Villain collaboration “didn’t resonate with customers” and weighed on Q3 results, highlighting underlying weak trends in its core body/core/home fragrance business, Lejuez said in his note to clients. This not only underscores weak traffic trends in off-peak periods but also raises the outlook for BBWI to rely more on price to drive demand in the near term.

“While we are hopeful new leadership can reinvigorate the core business through new product innovation, we are concerned that this will not have enough of an impact to drive a sales inflection in 2026,” he writes.

Lejuez also worries that retail partnerships could further erode store traffic.

During the company’s most recent earnings call with analysts, CEO Daniel Heaf intimated further partnerships could be in the works.

“The third part of our strategy is about winning in the marketplace. I fundamentally believe that you have to go to where the consumer is. You have to meet the consumer where they are so they can find, fall in love with, and purchase your product, this idea of just being a single stores-based retailer operating in your own 4 walls. I think that came to an end some time ago. So, if our aspiration is to grow share, we must grow share by winning in the marketplace."

While this strategy could raise BBWI’s top-line, it will exacerbate declining store traffic as customers will find it more convenient to shop online (and as BBWI’s own digital platform lags).

As a result, Citi expects management to issue FY26 EPS guidance of $2.10 to $2.25 versus the consensus estimate of $2.50 based on full year sales to be down low single-digits to mid-single digits. For the current quarter, Citi sees BBWI guiding sales down mid-single digits to high single digits versus the street consensus of 6% and EPS between $0.15 and $0.20 per share versus $0.28 consensus.

Bath & Body Works (BBWI) will release Q4 results before the market opens on March 4, expected to show an adjusted profit of $1.75 per share on $2.59B in sales.
